description: Add a post-release CI job that builds and pushes container images for released components when release-please creates a GitHub Release.

userStories.title: Add post-release CI job for artifact builds
userStories.description:
As a developer, I want a GitHub Actions workflow triggered by release events that builds and pushes container images for released components, so that tagged releases automatically produce deployable artifacts.

Add a job (or a new workflow) in .github/workflows/ that:
- Triggers on GitHub release published events
- Parses the release tag to determine which component was released (e.g., platform-v0.2.0 → component 'platform', path 'monorepo')
- Builds a container image for the released component using its Dockerfile (if one exists)
- Pushes the image to GitHub Container Registry (ghcr.io)
- Tags the image with the release version and 'latest'

Since no services with Dockerfiles exist yet, the workflow should:
1. Detect whether a Dockerfile exists for the released component
2. If yes: build and push the image
3. If no: skip gracefully with a log message

This is the bridge between 'a release was tagged' and 'an artifact exists in the registry'. The workflow should be extensible — as new components are added with Dockerfiles, they automatically get built on release.

Component-to-path mapping should be derived from release-please-config.json so there's a single source of truth.
acceptanceCriteria: A GitHub Actions workflow exists that triggers on release published events
The workflow parses the release tag to extract component name and version
The workflow reads release-please-config.json to map component names to paths
If a Dockerfile exists at the component path, the workflow builds and pushes to ghcr.io
If no Dockerfile exists, the workflow skips gracefully with a log message
Container images are tagged with both the version and 'latest'
The workflow YAML is valid and follows the existing workflow conventions in .github/workflows/
The workflow has appropriate permissions (packages: write for ghcr.io)
